userStories.title: Add context.fileInjection config flag and disable auto-detect by default
userStories.description: Currently nax auto-detects context files by running git grep on story title keywords (src/context/auto-detect.ts) and injects their content into agent prompts. The config flag context.autoDetect.enabled defaults to true. With MCP-equipped agents (Claude Code + code graph MCP), this pre-injection is redundant and wastes tokens. We need a top-level config flag context.fileInjection: 'keyword' | 'disabled' that defaults to 'disabled'. The existing context.autoDetect config block remains for backward compat when fileInjection is set to 'keyword'.
acceptanceCriteria: src/config/schemas.ts: ContextConfigSchema includes fileInjection with z.enum(['keyword', 'disabled']).default('disabled')
src/context/builder.ts: addSourceFileElements() skips auto-detect entirely when config.context.fileInjection === 'disabled' OR is undefined (treat missing as disabled)
src/context/builder.ts: also skips reading explicit story.contextFiles when fileInjection === 'disabled' (full injection bypass)
src/context/builder.ts: existing behavior (auto-detect + explicit contextFiles) preserved when fileInjection === 'keyword'

Unit tests in test/unit/context/file-injection.test.ts: disabled mode -> no file elements added; keyword mode -> auto-detect runs; keyword mode with explicit contextFiles -> files injected; missing fileInjection (undefined) -> treated as disabled

userStories.title: Document context.fileInjection in nax config --explain
userStories.description: Users need to discover the context.fileInjection config option and understand when to use 'keyword' vs 'disabled'. Update nax config --explain (src/cli/config.ts) to include a 'context' section. Default is 'disabled' because MCP-aware agents pull context on-demand; 'keyword' preserves legacy git-grep injection for non-MCP agents.
acceptanceCriteria: nax config --explain output includes a 'context' section with fileInjection documented
Documentation explains: default 'disabled' for MCP-aware agents; 'keyword' for legacy git-grep injection
Test in test/integration/cli/cli-config-context-explain.test.ts: spawn 'nax config --explain', assert stdout contains 'fileInjection', 'keyword', 'disabled'

userStories.title: Unit tests for context file injection modes
userStories.description: Ensure the context builder correctly handles all fileInjection modes. Tests verify: no file I/O in disabled mode, auto-detect runs in keyword mode, backward compat for legacy config.
acceptanceCriteria: test/unit/context/file-injection.test.ts covers: (1) fileInjection 'disabled' -> no git grep spawned; (2) fileInjection 'keyword' with matching files -> files injected; (3) fileInjection 'keyword' but autoDetect.enabled: false -> no auto-detect, explicit contextFiles still injected; (4) fileInjection undefined -> treated as 'disabled'; (5) explicit story.contextFiles with fileInjection 'disabled' -> NOT injected
Tests use mock for autoDetectContextFiles to verify call/no-call without spawning processes
All 5 cases pass with bun test test/unit/context/file-injection.test.ts
